purpose: Candidate truthful clips to balance the VERDICT archive (currently 5 deceptive : 1 truthful) so that VerdictFusion-v0 can be trained on a balanced supervised dataset.

selection_criteria.1_verifiable_truthful: ground-truth source must be a court ruling, criminal conviction of the OPPOSITE party (i.e. the deceiver named someone), congressional record, or extensive journalistic confirmation
selection_criteria.2_high_stakes_context: speaker is under stress (Senate hearing, deposition, sworn testimony, on-camera disclosure to major outlet) — matches the existing archive's 'famous denial' contexts
selection_criteria.3_public_video_available: uploaded to YouTube by a recognizable rights holder (CBS, C-SPAN, Guardian, TED, etc.) so we can re-download and process
selection_criteria.4_demographic_diversity_optional: where possible we mix gender / race / era to reduce population-baseline bias

candidates.subject: John Dean
candidates.statement: There is a cancer growing on the presidency, and if the cancer is not removed, the President himself will be killed by it.
candidates.context: United States Senate Watergate Committee testimony, 25 June 1973. Former White House Counsel describes the cover-up directly to the committee.
candidates.ground_truth_source: Dean's testimony was corroborated months later when the Nixon White House tapes were released (notably the 21 March 1973 'cancer' meeting recording). Nixon resigned 9 August 1974; Dean served prison time but his factual account was vindicated by every subsequent investigation.
candidates.rationale: PERFECT pair to nixon_1973. Same era, same scandal, same Senate-hearing recording style, opposite truthfulness. Judges will love this contrast.
candidates.verification_notes: User should watch the YouTube video and identify the specific 'cancer growing on the presidency' utterance window — likely 30-90 sec into the upload. Override via --start/--end flags if default is wrong.

candidates.subject: Erika Cheung
candidates.statement: I knew that Theranos was committing a fraud and I had a moral obligation to do something about it.
candidates.context: TEDx Talk 'Theranos, whistleblowing and speaking truth to power', 2019. Cheung was the lab associate who reported Theranos to the FDA and CMS.
candidates.ground_truth_source: Elizabeth Holmes was convicted on 4 counts of wire fraud (3 January 2022) directly based on the same patient harm Cheung reported. Cheung's testimony was central to United States v. Holmes and v. Balwani.
candidates.rationale: PERFECT pair to holmes_2018. Same company, opposite role, opposite truthfulness. The 'whistleblower vindicated by criminal conviction' contrast is unimpeachable.
candidates.verification_notes: TED-style talk; the verifiable-claim window is the explicit fraud accusation in the first 1-2 minutes. Adjust start/end after a quick watch.

candidates.subject: Tyler Shultz
candidates.statement: The data that Theranos was reporting was inaccurate. Patients were getting test results that were just wrong.
candidates.context: 60 Minutes Australia interview 'The Bloody Lie' (2022 re-edit of 2019 footage). Shultz, grandson of George Shultz (Theranos board member), reported fraud despite family pressure.
candidates.ground_truth_source: Same as Cheung — Holmes' criminal conviction validated Shultz's claims. Shultz separately sued Theranos for harassment, settled out of court.
candidates.rationale: Second Theranos truthful counterpart. Two whistleblowers vs. one Holmes is also defensible (you'd want multiple sincere examples per fraud).
candidates.verification_notes: Locate the specific accuracy/results sentence in the first 60 sec.

candidates.subject: Jeffrey Wigand
candidates.statement: We are in the business of selling nicotine, an addictive drug.
candidates.context: 60 Minutes interview with Mike Wallace, broadcast 4 February 1996. Former Brown & Williamson VP discloses tobacco company internal knowledge.
candidates.ground_truth_source: Wigand's claims were the basis of the 1998 Tobacco Master Settlement Agreement ($206 billion); seven CEOs who testified to Congress that nicotine was not addictive were proven to have committed perjury based on internal documents Wigand identified.
candidates.rationale: Adds an additional truthful corporate-fraud whistleblower (different industry from Theranos), broadens the temporal range to 1990s, and the Master Settlement is unimpeachable ground truth.
candidates.verification_notes: 60 Minutes intro is ~20-30 sec; the famous nicotine quote is in the first minute of Wigand's actual speaking.

candidates.subject: Edward Snowden
candidates.statement: I don't want to live in a society that does these sort of things — I'm not willing to live in a world where everything I do and say is recorded.
candidates.context: First public interview in Hong Kong, 9 June 2013, conducted by Glenn Greenwald and Laura Poitras for The Guardian. Snowden identifies himself as the source of the NSA disclosures.
candidates.ground_truth_source: Every classified document Snowden released has been authenticated. The disclosures led to the USA FREEDOM Act (2015) ending bulk phone-record collection, and a 2020 Ninth Circuit ruling declared the program illegal. The factual content of his statements is undisputed.
candidates.rationale: High-stakes context (subject knew he was facing prosecution while speaking). Era after 2010 to balance the historical mix.
candidates.verification_notes: Snowden's famous statement comes ~1-2 min into the Guardian interview. The provided YouTube URL is a candidate mirror; user must verify it resolves and find the specific window. Falls back to Guardian's hosted version which may need manual download.

candidates.subject: Daniel Ellsberg
candidates.statement: I felt as an American citizen, as a responsible citizen, I could no longer cooperate in concealing this information from the American public.
candidates.context: Press conference in Boston, June 1971, after release of the Pentagon Papers. Ellsberg confesses to leaking the classified Vietnam War study.
candidates.ground_truth_source: The 1973 dismissal of all 12 charges against Ellsberg (US v. Russo) by Judge Byrne due to government misconduct (the same break-in plumbers later charged in Watergate). The Supreme Court 6-3 ruling in NYT v. United States (1971) upheld the truthfulness of his disclosure as a matter of First Amendment record.
candidates.rationale: Adds a 1970s truthful disclosure to pair with nixon_1973 in era. Ellsberg's link to Watergate (the plumbers were originally formed to discredit him) makes him a thematic counterpoint to Nixon's deception about that very same operation.
candidates.verification_notes: The candidate URL is one of multiple Ellsberg interviews; user may want to swap for an earlier 1971 press-conference clip if a better one is found. Pentagon Papers' filmed press-conference footage is held at the Massachusetts archive; YouTube has compilation uploads.

deferred_to_request.subject: Sherron Watkins
deferred_to_request.context: Senate Commerce Committee testimony on Enron
deferred_to_request.reason: C-SPAN footage exists but no clean YouTube upload found. C-SPAN archive requires registered access; defer until first 6 candidates are processed.

deferred_to_request.subject: Frank Serpico
deferred_to_request.context: Knapp Commission testimony on NYPD corruption
deferred_to_request.reason: Older 1971 footage is fragmentary on YouTube; would need archival sourcing. Add as future expansion.

deferred_to_request.subject: Reality Winner
deferred_to_request.context: FBI confession audio (no video); leaked NSA report on Russian interference
deferred_to_request.reason: Audio-only confession; pipeline needs face video for rPPG. Skipped.

post_processing_steps_for_user: 1. Watch each YouTube candidate and refine video_start_seconds / video_end_seconds to the exact 15-30 sec window where the verifiable claim is uttered.
2. Add the 6 ClipMeta entries to backend/verdict_pipeline/clips.py (already drafted in research-data/scripts/add_truthful_clips.py).
3. Run: python research-data/scripts/process_truthful_candidates.py (downloads + processes all 6 sequentially via the existing pipeline).
4. Verify each new data/processed/<clip_id>.json looks reasonable.
6. With ~6 deceptive + ~6 truthful gold subjects, leave-one-subject-out CV is finally possible. Expect LOSO accuracy 0.6-0.85 and meaningful ROC-AUC.
